stemHtml: On a transatlantic flight, the captain asks the First Officer why a final step climb from FL340 to FL380 ten minutes before top of descent might not be an efficient use of the fuel. Select a possible explanation.

options.a: A 4000 ft climb might consume more fuel than would be saved by being at the higher altitude for a short period.
options.b: The headwind present at FL 340 might be more favourable than the still air conditions at FL380.
options.c: Even in still air, cruising at the higher optimum level would decrease the specific range.
options.d: There might be stronger tailwinds at FL380.
